docstring.1751823659459:
This `job` is used to `write storage` from `memory` into `storage`.

NOTE: <red>If you modify the `file` in `disk`, then you need to issue `/fuji reload` as soon as possible.</red>
NOTE: <red>The `disk` will be `overridden` when `fire` this job.</red>
docstring.1751823676896:
Fuji is composed by `core` and `module`.
The `core` config affects `all` modules.
The `module` config only affects that specific module.
docstring.1751823688579:
Fuji is designed to be fully-modular.
All modules is `disabled` by default.
You can modify the `enable` field to enable a module <red>after a server re-start</red>.
<b>Remember to `restart` the server to apply the modification.

Issue `/fuji inspect modules` to see the module status.
docstring.1751823731455: Fuji will back up the `config/fuji` dir before it loads any module.
docstring.1751823761222:
The scheduler related options.
The `scheduler` system is used to run `jobs`.
docstring.1751823767950:
The logger level for `quartz` library.
The level is recommended to be higher than `WARN`, to prevent console spam.
Acceptable levels: ALL < TRACE < DEBUG < INFO < WARN < ERROR < FATAL < OFF
docstring.1751823774480: How many `backup files` should we keep?
docstring.1751823780583:
The `paths` that should be skipped when backup.
The `path` is resolved and related to `config/fuji/` dir.
docstring.1751823787824:
The `default language` used by Fuji.
The language files are located in `config/fuji/lang` dir.
docstring.1751823793347:
Fuji defines commands into 2 groups, for different users.
One group for `normal user`, these commands require `level 0 permission` to use.
One group for `admin user`, these commands require `level 4 permission` to use.

If you want to define the permission of commands by yourself,
you can enable `this` option.
And use `command_permission` module to define permission for each command.
docstring.1751823800414:
Force disable `all` modules.
Used to test the compatibility between `fuji` and `other mods`.
docstring.1751823806770:
Should we log the `debug` level messages into the `console`?
This option can be changed using `/fuji debug` command.
docstring.1751823813518: Should we print the user guide in the console on server startup?
docstring.1751823830414: The times to play this sound.
docstring.1751823834755: The interval between each sound play.
docstring.1751823848934: The `center x` used as the `origin` for `rtp`.

docstring.1751823883726: The `relative y distance` is ranged `(minY, maxY]`.
docstring.1751823896496: Max try times before aborting a `rtp` request.
docstring.1751823907693: Attached `tag name` list for this `configuration section`.
docstring.1751823921153: The max distance to interrupt this request.

docstring.1751823938547: Interrupt this request if player in combat.

docstring.1751823954950: The `pattern` used to replace the `matched target string`.
docstring.1751823961060: This `job` is used to clean up the `TTL Map` data structure, and remove `expired entries`.
docstring.1751823967217:
Provides `/json` command.
A powerful tool to edit json file.
docstring.1751823973159:
Provides `/foreach` command.
If a command is only targets on single player, you can use `/foreach` to apply it for each online player.
docstring.1751823980406: Execute a command targeted on single-player for each player online.
docstring.1751823988812: Provides `/run` command, to run a command with context.
docstring.1751823999061: Execute a command as a player.
docstring.1751824003937: Execute a command as a player with fake-op.
docstring.1751824017011: When the command is executed? (If not executed, the time is null)
docstring.1751824024333:
This module provides the `/when-online \<player\> \<cmd\>` command.
To execute a specified command `exactly once` when the target player is `online`:
1. If the target player is `online` now, the command will be executed `at once`.
2. If the target player is `offline` now, the command will be executed `when the player online`.

Besides, you use `/when-online list` to manage `submitted commands`.

For example:
1. `/when-online Steve give %player:name% minecraft:apple 3`
2. `/when-online Alex delay 8 say Hi %player:name%`
docstring.1751824713488: Provides `/one-of` command, to pick a random command from commands, and execute it.
docstring.1751824718640: One-of command randomly pick one of commands and execute it as console.
docstring.1751824730196:
Provides `/chain` command.
It allows you to run another 2 commands.
The first command is any command.
The second command is the chain command.
docstring.1751824736793: Chain commands and executes them in sequence, the chain will break if the previous one command fails.
docstring.1751824750326: Only the `console` can use the `/shell` command?
docstring.1751824756091: Only these `players` can use the `/shell` command?
docstring.1751824777515:
Provides `/shell` command.

docstring.1751824793427: Provides a unified attachment facility, to attach any data to any object.

The `key` is `translatable key`.

If the `value` is `null`, then it means `cancel` the sending of this message.
docstring.1751824926635:
By default, any player can use `all style tags`.
Enable this option requires the player to has `corresponding permission` to use that `style tag`.

For example, to use `<red>` tag, requires `fuji.style.sign.red` permission.
docstring.1751824932882: This module allows you to use color tags in `sign` blocks.
docstring.1751824937869:
By default, any player can use `all style tags`.
Enable this option requires the player to has `corresponding permission` to use that `style tag`.

For example, to use `<red>` tag, requires `fuji.style.anvil.red` permission.
docstring.1751824946979: This module allows you to use color tags in `anvil` screen.
docstring.1751824965598:
This module allows you to write commands in `sign` block.
And then click to execute commands.
docstring.1751824970923: Allow you to edit player's inventory and enderchest.
docstring.1751825006787: This `job` is used to `update` the nametag for each player.
docstring.1751825018627: Customize the nametag above the players.
docstring.1751825104871: Jump to the position looking at.
docstring.1751825123433: Teleport to the top of your current position.

docstring.1751825147630:
This is a convenient command, to teleport `others` to `you`.
Similar to `/tp @a <player>`.

For example:
1. `/tphere Steve` to teleport `Steve` to `you`.
2. `/tphere @a` to teleport `all online players` to `you`.
docstring.1751825152795: Fill the `health` and `hunger` for the player.
docstring.1751825164823: Set food level, saturation level and exhaustion to healthy state.
docstring.1751825174228: Allow you to set the target point of a compass item.
docstring.1751825179946: Let the compass in hand track a specified position.
docstring.1751825185305: Let the compass in hand track a specified player.
docstring.1751825191491: Let the compass in hand track nothing.
docstring.1751825196038: Set the count of item in hand to max count.
docstring.1751825231545: Toggle the fly state of the player.
docstring.1751825242047:
Provides `/tppos` command.
A `unified` and `powerful` teleport command.

For example:
1. `/tppos --z 64 --x 32 --y 128` to teleport to `a specified position`
2. `/tppos --others Steve` to specify the `target player`.
3. `/tppos offline Alex` to teleport to the `offline position of Alex`.
4. `/tppos --dimension` to specify the `target dimension`, and start `a random tp`.
5. `/tppos --minRange 1000 --maxRange 2000` to specify the setup for `a random tp`.
6. `/tppos here @a` to teleport `all online players` to `you`.
docstring.1751825322575: is the shape of rtp circle or square
docstring.1751825355777: Teleport to the offline position of a player.
docstring.1751825360963:
Teleport `others` to `you`.

For example:
1. `/tppos here Steve` to teleport `Steve` to `you`.
2. `/tppos here @a` to teleport `all online players` to `you`.
docstring.1751825375878: Reply the player who recently /msg or /tell you.

docstring.1751825625507:
This module allows you to ban `types of actions` for players.

Currently supported types are:
1. Break a specified block.
2. Place a specified block.
3. Interact with a specified item.
4. Interact with a specified block.
5. Interact with a specified entity.
docstring.1751825634067: The `display name` of an afk player in `tab` list.
docstring.1751825643038:
Afk checker is `triggered` periodically.
To check whether a player has any input action.
And mark the player as `afk` if there is no `action` for too long.
docstring.1751825650915: The `cron` expression used to trigger `afk checker`.
docstring.1751825654901: Define commands to run on afk events.

docstring.1751826206965:
This module provides special effects for afk player:
1. Invulnerable: the afk player is immune to all damage.
2. Targetable: the afk player can not be targeted by a hostile entity.
3. Moveable: the afk player can not be moved.
docstring.1751826238005:
This module provides:
1. Afk detection: If a player idle too long, we will mark it as afk state.
2. Afk event: Execute commands when a player enters or leaves afk state.
3. Afk name customization: For a afk player, we can customize its display name in tab list.
docstring.1751826271499: Test if a player is in afk state.
docstring.1751826288031: This module allows you to define `regex` to rewrite the command line a player issued.
docstring.1751826302190:

The defined alies command will redirect to the existing command node.

docstring.1751826766512: For the `matched permission string`, we directly return the `pre-defined` permission test result.
docstring.1751826769068:
You can define `rules` to handle `special case`.
If the `command permission` is `matched` by the `rule`.
We return the pre-defined `permission test result` directly, without asking the luckperms.

The `rules` can be used to handle `special case`.
For example, if you enable the `permission implicitly inheritance` feature, and grant a `root permission` like `fuji.permission.fly`.
You want to allow the players to use `/fly`, whose permission is `fuji.permission.fly`.
But you don't want the players to use `/fly others <player>`, whose permission is `fuji.permission.fly.others.others`.
In this case, you just enable the `permission implicitly inheritance` feature, and grant the `root permission`, then use rules to `exclude` special cases.

NOTE: <red>Pre-defined rules only applied to non-operator players.</red>

The unit of `age` is `game tick` (20 ticks = 1 sec).
